name: creative_theme_definition
content: Strong creative work starts with a clear theme, audience, mood, and intended impression.

name: creative_concept_motif
content: A motif gives a project recognizability by repeating an idea across visual, verbal, and structural details.

name: creative_divergence_convergence
content: Creative process benefits from separating divergence for idea generation and convergence for selection and refinement.

name: creative_contrast_balance
content: Contrast creates attention, but balance and hierarchy keep the message readable and coherent.

name: creative_reference_usage
content: References are most useful when they clarify mood and structure rather than being copied literally.

name: creative_storytelling_arc
content: A compelling concept usually has a hook, a clear point of view, and a memorable closing beat.

name: creative_editing_for_clarity
content: Editing often improves creative work more than adding more elements; removal strengthens coherence.

name: creative_multi_direction_exploration
content: Comparing two or three distinct directions helps teams discuss taste, risk, and positioning more effectively than polishing one option too early.
